E

Ascents of the Matterhorn

No.В of Ascent. Date. Names. Route taken. Remarks.
1865.
1 July 13вЃ вЂ“вЃ 15 Lord Francis Douglas.
D. Hadow.
Charles Hudson.
Edward Whymper.		 Zermatt
(or Northern route).		 GuidesвЃ вЂ”Michel Croz, Peter Taugwalder pГЁre, Peter Taugwalder fils. See Chapters XXI and XXII.
2 July 16вЃ вЂ“вЃ 18 Jean-Antoine Carrel.
J. Baptiste Bich.
AmГ© Gorret.
J.-Augustin Meynet.		 Breuil
(or Southern route).		 The first two named only ascended to the summit. See Appendix C.
1867.
3 Aug. 13вЃ вЂ“вЃ 15 F. Craufurd Grove. Breuil. GuidesвЃ вЂ”J.-A. Carrel, Salomon Meynet, and J. B. Bich.
4 Sept. 12вЃ вЂ“вЃ 14 Jos. Maquignaz.
J.-Pierre Maquignaz.
Victor Maquignaz.
Caesar Carrel.
J.-B. Carrel.		 Breuil. An easier route was discovered by this party than that taken upon July 17, 1865. The first two named only ascended to the summit. See Appendix C.
5 Oct. 1вЃ вЂ“вЃ 3 W. Leighton Jordan. Breuil. GuidesвЃ вЂ”the MaquignazвЂ™s just named, Caesar Carrel, and F. Ansermin. The MaquignazвЂ™s and Mr.В Jordan alone reached the summit.

8 Aug. 2вЃ вЂ“вЃ 4 O. Hoiler.
F. Thioly.		 Up Zermatt side and down Breuil side. Account given in hotel-book at Breuil is not very clear. Guides seem to have been J.-Jos. and Victor Maquignaz and Elie Pession.

This party was confined in the hut on the Italian side from the 18th to the 21st of August, by bad weather; and in descending upon the Zermatt side it was surprised by night before the cabane could be reached, and had to pass the night on the open mountainside.
